jokes.text: My greatest weakness is humility. I'm probably the most humble person in the whole world.
jokes.setup: Jack reading from his self-evaluation, claiming his greatest weakness is humility
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by claiming to be the most humble person in the world
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Jack's obliviousness to his own arrogance. The irony is immediate and sharp, with excellent economy of language.

jokes.text: What three words best describe me? The. Complete. Package. My greatest weakness... Is chocolate.
jokes.setup: Reading another employee's equally ridiculous self-evaluation
jokes.punchline: The mundane 'chocolate' weakness after the grandiose self-description
jokes.explanation: Solid contrast joke with good structure, though less character-specific than Jack's line.

jokes.text: When I used to live with Liz, I would videotape her sleeping and sell it to Japanese businessmen
jokes.setup: Jack noting that Jenna was surprisingly honest in her evaluation
jokes.punchline: Reveals an extremely disturbing and illegal activity presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that's perfectly in character for Jenna. The casual delivery of something so outrageous is masterfully crafted.

jokes.text: No wonder that tour group kept calling me 'sleep whore-San.'
jokes.setup: Liz processing the revelation about the Japanese businessmen
jokes.punchline: Connects a previously unexplained incident to Jenna's confession with a Japanese honorific pun
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that recontextualizes what seemed like a throwaway detail. Excellent economy and the 'San' suffix adds perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: I filled it out last night after mixing alcohol with prescription... exhaustion.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to excuse her honest self-evaluation
jokes.punchline: Sets up expectation of prescription drugs, delivers 'exhaustion' instead
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that plays on pharmaceutical language while being character-appropriate for Jenna's dramatics.

jokes.text: This is the biggest waste of time since NBC's diversity writing program. That was a good idea, but all of our actors are so white.
jokes.setup: Jack complaining about having to read evaluations
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about NBC's actual lack of diversity, delivered with characteristic Jack obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on network television delivered through Jack's character. Self-aware without breaking character.

jokes.text: Slap it to me, Daddy-O. Popo popped dookie down by the vacants.
jokes.setup: Tracy apparently trying to demonstrate diversity in writing
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical street slang that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy absurdism. The randomness works because it's so characteristic of his unpredictable contributions.

jokes.text: I was shooting blow darts at Maggie Gyllenhaal from a heating vent.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining how she met 'Christopher Nolan' at a premiere
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of attempted assault on a celebrity from an air vent
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deranged Jenna moment. The specific detail and casual delivery of something completely insane is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: He's directing a live-action Smurfs sequel, so I went to Times Square, did cartwheels like he asked...
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining Christopher Nolan's 'methods'
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of doing cartwheels in Times Square for a Smurfs movie
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup that shows Jenna's gullibility. The specificity of cartwheels adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: This isn't body paint. It's a spray they use to kill geese at airports.
jokes.setup: Jenna realizing she's been pranked and covered in blue
jokes.punchline: The paint is actually goose-killing chemicals
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that takes the prank from harmless to disturbing. The specificity of 'geese at airports' makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: Did you pass out in a goose costume at LaGuardia?
jokes.setup: Liz reacting to the goose spray revelation
jokes.punchline: Connects the dots with a specific, absurd scenario
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that creates a vivid, specific image. LaGuardia adds New York specificity.

jokes.text: It's called 'Desire,' but with a 'Z' instead of the second 'E.'
jokes.setup: Tracy introducing his new fragrance
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary spelling clarification for a simple substitution
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor playing on Tracy's tendency to over-explain obvious things.

jokes.text: The Knicks, a mop from a strip club, a carefree hobo, a crate with a new giraffe in it, and broccoli.
jokes.setup: Asked what the fragrance is supposed to smell like
jokes.punchline: Completely random list that makes no sense as a fragrance combination
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy absurdism at its finest. The specificity of 'carefree hobo' and 'crate with a new giraffe' is inspired.

jokes.text: I feel an old Jack-o-lantern from last Halloween.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming enhanced touch sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates his 'gift' by identifying something completely mundane and obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of expectations. Tracy's obliviousness to how unimpressive his 'gift' is makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who has the time, with work, family and hobbies and listing excuses?
jokes.setup: Asked why he hasn't been to a doctor
jokes.punchline: Includes 'listing excuses' as one of his time-consuming activities
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor where Tracy acknowledges his own excuse-making as an excuse. Good self-awareness without breaking character.

jokes.text: It smells like grandma's house at Christmas. That's when we found her dead on the toilet.
jokes.setup: Liz describing a pleasant Christmas memory smell
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts the nostalgia with a morbid detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect economy. The dark turn is unexpected and the specific detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Those look like hands to me.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Pete's evaluation gave him 'pause'
jokes.punchline: Pete misunderstanding 'pause' as 'paws'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that fits Pete's character but isn't particularly inspired. Functional comedy.

jokes.text: What if the Wright brothers had said, 'let's just keep making bicycles,' or Alexander windowblinds had said, 'no, I don't want to partially see outside'?
jokes.setup: Jack making an inspirational point about ambition
jokes.punchline: Invents 'Alexander windowblinds' as a historical figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment that perfectly captures Jack's tendency to make up 'facts' with complete confidence. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: The last time I tried to put myself out there, the girl turned out to be a cop.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his lack of ambition
jokes.punchline: Implies he was arrested while trying to meet women
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that adds to Pete's patheticness. The implication is funnier than explicit detail would be.

jokes.text: What? Like Frasier? But that's fun...
jokes.setup: Jack saying something is a farce
jokes.punchline: Pete misunderstanding and thinking of the comedy genre
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Pete's intellectual limitations. The Frasier reference feels authentic to his character.

jokes.text: My father was a congressman. I was valedictorian at St. Andrew's, an olympic archer, fourth guitarist in Loverboy... As a teenager.
jokes.setup: Pete listing his past achievements
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being in Loverboy as a teenager, then correcting himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect character work. The Loverboy detail is absurd but Pete's correction makes it believable.

jokes.text: George W. Bush, during his 'let's do coke and buy the Texas rangers' phase. But he made a decision to be the best president ever and then he was.
jokes.setup: Jack comparing Pete to Bush's wild phase
jokes.punchline: Sincerely believes Bush became the best president ever
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment showing his Republican delusions. The casual 'coke' reference followed by sincere praise is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: I can either drill a hole in the back of your head, and push it out with your brain... Okay, that sounds good.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman offering two medical options
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately accepting the obviously dangerous brain drilling option
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction. Dr. Spaceman's incompetence paired with Tracy's impulsiveness creates perfect absurd logic.

jokes.text: Being a doctor is exactly like the game 'Operation.' And there we go.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman performing the procedure
jokes.punchline: Compares real surgery to a children's board game
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman incompetence. The comparison is both absurd and terrifyingly plausible for his character.

jokes.text: Women staying quiet.
jokes.setup: Asked what else is from the '70s besides his decoder ring
jokes.punchline: Casually sexist comment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Fits Tracy's character but relies on outdated gender stereotypes. The casualness works but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: If my Foster mom saw it, she would make me sell it at a pawn shop to get our family a cheerio.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he hid the decoder ring
jokes.punchline: Single cheerio as the family's financial goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent exaggeration of poverty that's both absurd and character-appropriate. The singular 'cheerio' is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Sometimes I think the stories of your childhood poverty are exaggerated, but continue.
jokes.setup: Reacting to Tracy's cheerio story
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the absurdity while playing straight man
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary that acknowledges the absurdity without breaking the scene. Dr. Spaceman as unlikely voice of reason.

jokes.text: I was talking to my lover on my Bluetooth. One moment, suki.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman appearing distracted during medical procedure
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having phone sex during surgery
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman unprofessionalism. The timing during surgery makes it more absurd.

jokes.text: Please try to get it on video for my nephew's funny website.
jokes.setup: Warning Tracy about extreme reactions to regaining smell
jokes.punchline: Asks to film patient's potential medical distress for internet comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment showing his complete lack of medical ethics. The 'funny website' detail adds authenticity.

jokes.text: May I suggest messily eating an orange while I photograph it?
jokes.setup: Someone offering Liz vitamin C
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and sexual suggestion involving fruit
jokes.explanation: Weird and specific enough to be memorable. The photography element makes it creepier and funnier.

jokes.text: The Bible says it's wrong, but it's the surprise hit of the season on ABC, so I don't know.
jokes.setup: Asked about revenge
jokes.punchline: Conflates biblical teachings with TV show success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic mixing religious morality with TV ratings. The uncertainty in 'so I don't know' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Who even remembers 2007?
jokes.setup: Jenna saying she was young when she got a tattoo
jokes.punchline: Treating 2007 as ancient history
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna self-centeredness and drama. The specificity of 2007 makes it feel authentically shallow.

jokes.text: In this big trash bag, I see everyone's mistakes, their embarrassing secrets, their dreams they don't dare share with anyone.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his role as janitor
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of garbage collection as psychological insight
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment where Kenneth finds profound meaning in mundane work. The escalating poetry is perfectly Kenneth.

jokes.text: They call me 'the natural.' I'm so good, the other janitors just sit in the basement all day getting drunk and laughing about this idiot they know.
jokes.setup: Kenneth boasting about his janitorial skills
jokes.punchline: Reveals the other janitors are mocking him, not respecting him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth obliviousness. His pride in being exploited while others slack off is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Like a couple of Jews watching The Daily Show.
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on him and Pete laughing together
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific ethnic stereotype about comedy consumption
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack casual stereotyping delivered with complete confidence. The specificity makes it character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Including the one that looked like a terrified Asian man?
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning TR's hunting trophies
jokes.punchline: Pete questioning one specific disturbing trophy
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that fits the show's willingness to go to uncomfortable places. Pete's specificity adds to the wrongness.

jokes.text: Except of course, uh, hide and seek.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming boxing was the first sport man invented
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction with something obviously older and non-violent
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jack's pseudo-intellectual moment. The hesitation suggests he knows how silly the correction is.

jokes.text: Actually she took me that first night.
jokes.setup: Jack's macho speech about dragging Paula to his cave
jokes.punchline: Pete's pathetic admission of being dominated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete moment undermining Jack's masculine fantasy. The matter-of-fact delivery makes his emasculation funnier.

jokes.text: For God's sake, Hornberger, the dummy is winning!
jokes.setup: Pete apparently losing a fight with a punching dummy
jokes.punchline: Jack's exasperated commentary on Pete's pathetic performance
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy implied through dialogue. The idea of losing to an inanimate object is perfectly Pete.

jokes.text: I'm submitting to its strength. It's the Alpha.
jokes.setup: Jack asking why Pete is kissing the dummy
jokes.punchline: Pete treating the dummy as a dominant opponent
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Pete's patheticness. Applying alpha/beta dynamics to a punching dummy is absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: ♪ Perfume and roses and strong halitosis ♪ ♪ Powders and flowers and spicy clam chowders ♪
jokes.setup: Tracy singing about his newly regained sense of smell
jokes.punchline: Mixing pleasant and unpleasant scents in rhyming couplets
jokes.explanation: Fun musical moment that captures Tracy's joy and randomness. The mix of pleasant and gross scents is characteristic.

jokes.text: Daddy?
jokes.setup: Tracy's joyful song about smells
jokes.punchline: Abrupt emotional shift suggesting he smells something that reminds him of his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tonal shift from comedy to pathos in one word. Sets up major emotional storyline while maintaining comedic context.

jokes.text: She writes the songs herself. When she can't cry any more, she writes!
jokes.setup: Frank defending his Taylor Swift fandom
jokes.punchline: Overly emotional description of Swift's creative process
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's unexpected sensitivity. The dramatic language contrasts with his usual persona.

jokes.text: You're the man who took professional boudoir photographs and then tried to mail them to Parade magazine columnist Marilyn Vos Savant.
jokes.setup: Toofer mocking Frank
jokes.punchline: Liz revealing Toofer's own embarrassing secret
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and weird. The combination of boudoir photos and an intellectual columnist is perfectly Toofer.

jokes.text: I happen to think that smart is sexy. Our foreplay would be puzzle-solving.
jokes.setup: Toofer defending his attraction to Vos Savant
jokes.punchline: Overly literal description of intellectual foreplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toofer response showing his intellectual pretensions. The puzzle-solving detail is both funny and believable for his character.

jokes.text: You don't matter.
jokes.setup: Lutz asking what his embarrassing secret will be
jokes.punchline: Liz's brutal dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered cruelty with maximum economy. The matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier and meaner.

jokes.text: You don't do 22 episodes of 'Celebrity outhouse' with Lorena Bobbitt without learning a few things.
jokes.setup: Liz threatening the writers
jokes.punchline: References an absurd reality show with a notorious figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake reality show that sounds plausible for this universe. The Bobbitt reference adds dark specificity.

jokes.text: Midnight symphony. You are the conductor of your own proud African orchestra.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Tracy's hair pomade
jokes.punchline: Dotcom's poetic description of the product's marketing
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment for Dotcom showing his appreciation for cultural pride. The marketing copy sounds authentic and inspiring.

jokes.text: You didn't go to the store for milk and heroin and then never come back.
jokes.setup: Tracy happy that someone stayed with him
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of heroin as a grocery item
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about abandonment with the absurd pairing of milk and heroin. The casualness makes Tracy's trauma both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Corn is not the only thing he popped, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Orville Redenbacher as ultra-confident
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about the popcorn mogul
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that fits Jack's tendency toward inappropriate comments. Not particularly clever but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Great men like Richard Nixon, the 1980 olympic hockey team, my good friend John Rambo.
jokes.setup: Jack listing men with nothing left to lose
jokes.punchline: Includes fictional character as if he's real and personal friend
jokes.explanation: Great Jack moment - treats fictional character as personal acquaintance with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: I was mugged by what I'm pretty sure was two five-year-olds in a trench coat.
jokes.setup: Pete describing how bad his life has become
jokes.punchline: Absurd mugging scenario that suggests either incompetent criminals or Pete's own patheticness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image that perfectly captures Pete's patheticness. The uncertainty in 'pretty sure' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: No, I'm quoting myself talking to Bruce Willis.
jokes.setup: Pete asking if Jack is quoting Lord of the Rings
jokes.punchline: Jack claims to have given the same speech to a major movie star
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack ego moment where he claims credit for movie dialogue. The specificity of Bruce Willis adds believability to the absurdity.

jokes.text: If it isn't tweedle-Dee and tweedle-black.
jokes.setup: Liz confronting Frank and Toofer
jokes.punchline: Racial twist on the classic literary reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay though not particularly inspired. Works as character moment showing Liz's occasional edge.

jokes.text: If you can't stand the heat, get off of Mickey Rourke's sex grill.
jokes.setup: Writers saying they need to talk about what she did
jokes.punchline: Bizarre twist on a common saying involving Mickey Rourke
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird twist on a cliché. The specificity of Mickey Rourke and 'sex grill' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: He is estranged from his son Kellan Lutz from Twilight.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Lutz has self-esteem issues
jokes.punchline: Claims real actor Kellan Lutz is Lutz's son
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke connecting a background character to a real actor. The family connection adds absurd backstory.

jokes.text: I want to be famous to make people love me because I hate myself. The Jenna I talk to at night in the mirror thinks I should kill myself. Also, I caused that Italian cruise ship to crash.
jokes.setup: Reading from Jenna's honest self-evaluation
jokes.punchline: Escalates from psychological issues to claiming responsibility for a real disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that goes from relatable self-loathing to completely insane. The cruise ship reference is perfectly random and dark.

jokes.text: This is my Jimmy Fallon impression. Awesome, awesome, I love it! It's my favorite!
jokes.setup: Tracy doing a celebrity impression
jokes.punchline: Generic enthusiasm that could apply to any late-night host
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about late-night TV enthusiasm. Not particularly sharp but fits Tracy's character.

jokes.text: It's a California Kong, which is two California kings tied together with gorilla leather.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his bed
jokes.punchline: Absurdly large bed with disturbing animal product detail
jokes.explanation: Great wealth comedy with dark undertones. The gorilla leather detail adds disturbing specificity that's perfectly Tracy.

jokes.text: Do you know what she does in her underwear? Something I once did at the oscars that caused Daryl Hannah to throw up on me.
jokes.setup: Asked about baby Virginia
jokes.punchline: Compares baby's bathroom habits to his own Oscar incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that connects baby's natural functions to Tracy's past debauchery. The Daryl Hannah detail adds perfect specificity.

jokes.text: John Adams and Mitt Romney enter.
jokes.setup: Tracy working on the show set in 1776
jokes.punchline: Anachronistically includes modern politician in historical setting
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Tracy's historical confusion. The casual anachronism is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That girl at Starbucks smiles at everybody, Lutz. Everybody! She doesn't want you to kiss her.
jokes.setup: Lutz apparently talking to himself
jokes.punchline: Self-directed harsh reality check about misreading social cues
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing Lutz's self-awareness of his social failures. The repetition adds emphasis to his desperation.

jokes.text: Ms. Maroney, judging is for God and his angels. So, yes, you are.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if she's the worst person Kenneth knows
jokes.punchline: Kenneth claims not to judge then immediately judges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment with flawless structure. The immediate contradiction is delivered with beautiful sincerity.

jokes.text: To use a technical janitorial term, total ass.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing the smell of the writers' trash
jokes.punchline: Claims crude language is technical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth humor combining his innocent demeanor with unexpected profanity. The 'technical term' setup works well.

jokes.text: Or I could be 6 feet under... In the subterranean paradise we built to escape the poor.
jokes.setup: Jack discussing uncertainty about his future
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate rich person bunker plans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment revealing his class consciousness and privilege. The casual mention of avoiding the poor is expertly delivered.

jokes.text: We took photos of you and sold them to a garbage fetish website.
jokes.setup: Revealing they knew Liz was going through garbage
jokes.punchline: Monetized her humiliation through a specific fetish site
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the writers' entrepreneurial pettiness. The specificity of a garbage fetish site adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Kellan Lutz isn't my son. He's my grandnephew and we're very close.
jokes.setup: Revealing the Kellan Lutz story was fake
jokes.punchline: Actually has a close relationship with the actor, just different family connection
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds unexpected depth to the throwaway joke. The 'very close' detail makes it sweeter.

jokes.text: Thanks to you guys, I'm the fourth worst.
jokes.setup: Jenna realizing she's not actually the worst person
jokes.punchline: Celebrates moving up in the rankings of awfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna logic finding positivity in being terrible. The specific ranking shows her competitive nature even about being awful.

jokes.text: Kellan Lutz isn't my son. He's my grandnephew and we're very close.
jokes.setup: Revelation that previous story was fake
jokes.punchline: Turns out relationship is real but different and still weird
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup. The 'very close' part maintains the weird factor.

jokes.text: Like some sort of American Barack Obama.
jokes.setup: Jack apologizing for telling Pete how to live
jokes.punchline: Describes Obama as specifically American, missing the redundancy
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment showing his tendency toward redundant descriptors. The casual Obama reference fits his political awareness.

jokes.text: It looks like a swastika made out of penises.
jokes.setup: Pete revealing his birthmark
jokes.punchline: Horrifically specific description of the worst possible birthmark
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant visual description that combines the two most inappropriate symbols possible. Instant classic dark comedy.

jokes.text: Do you know what it's like to try to have sex wearing a child's little orphan Annie wig?
jokes.setup: Pete complaining about his appearance
jokes.punchline: Horrifically specific image of his sex life
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing and specific image that captures Pete's complete emasculation. The child's wig detail makes it worse and funnier.

jokes.text: Do you know what it's like to try to have sex wearing a child's little orphan Annie wig?
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his recent problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetically specific sexual impediment
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. The specificity of 'child's little orphan Annie wig' makes it devastatingly funny.

jokes.text: 'Cause so much of me has died.
jokes.setup: Liz asking why Tracy is so heavy
jokes.punchline: Metaphysical explanation for physical weight
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd Tracy logic connecting emotional death to physical weight. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Pop dookie down by the vacants?
jokes.setup: Liz asking what Tracy needs to do to make her proud
jokes.punchline: References his earlier nonsensical street slang
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier absurdist moment. Shows Tracy's confusion about what's expected of him.

jokes.text: My bitch ex-wife would be a millionaire. May she rest in peace.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman talking about how much he gets paid for procedures
jokes.punchline: Casually implies he killed his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman dark comedy delivered with complete casualness. The 'rest in peace' makes the implication clear and disturbing.

jokes.text: A magnolia tree in spring, the towels at a miss Hawaiian tropic contest, the sweat of a terrified Webster as you load him into a Cannon.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing things he hasn't smelled yet
jokes.punchline: List goes from beautiful to bizarre to disturbing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list that gets progressively darker. 'Terrified Webster' being loaded into a cannon is inspired.

jokes.text: Get a haircut, or I'm gonna shove you up a black guy's nose.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman about to insert a talking pencil topper
jokes.punchline: The pencil topper threatens its own fate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where the object acknowledges its destiny. The self-awareness while maintaining absurdity is expertly done.

jokes.text: Goodbye only evidence tying me to my ex-wife's disappearance.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman saying goodbye to the pencil topper
jokes.punchline: Reveals the pencil topper is evidence of murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the Dr. Spaceman murder subplot. The casualness of disposing of evidence is brilliantly disturbing.

jokes.text: Or whatever NBC is in five years. A T-shirt company probably?
jokes.setup: Jack talking about working for NBC in his evaluation
jokes.punchline: Predicts NBC's decline into irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on network television's decline. The T-shirt company prediction is both specific and plausible.

jokes.text: In five years, I'll probably be dead and no one will even notice, and yes, this is my second attempt to writing my self evaluation.
jokes.setup: Pete's self-evaluation response
jokes.punchline: Morbid prediction followed by admission of multiple drafts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete despair with the meta-element of needing multiple attempts. The specificity about being unnoticed adds to his patheticness.
